# Bloomneo — Machine-Readable Reference

> This is the root llms.txt for the Bloomneo developer framework.
> It indexes all three packages and provides quick-start context for AI coding agents.
> For full API references, follow the links to each package's llms.txt below.

## What is Bloomneo?

Bloomneo is an open-source developer toolkit for building fast, modular, AI-first apps.
Three packages work together — or independently:

- **@bloomneo/appkit** — Node.js backend toolkit (12 modules: auth, database, cache, queue, storage, email, events, logging, config, security, util)
- **@bloomneo/uikit** — React component library (45+ components, 5 OKLCH themes, cross-platform)
- **@bloomneo/bloom** — CLI scaffolding (web, desktop via Electron, mobile via Capacitor)

## Package references

| Package | Version | llms.txt | AGENTS.md |
|---|---|---|---|
| `@bloomneo/appkit` | 1.5.2 | [appkit/llms.txt](appkit/llms.txt) | [appkit/AGENTS.md](appkit/AGENTS.md) |
| `@bloomneo/uikit`  | 1.5.1 | [uikit/llms.txt](uikit/llms.txt)   | [uikit/AGENTS.md](uikit/AGENTS.md)   |
| `@bloomneo/bloom`  | 1.5.3 | [bloom/llms.txt](bloom/llms.txt)   | —                                     |

## The one pattern that matters (AppKit)

Every AppKit module follows `xxxClass.get()`. There are no constructors, no factories.

```ts
import { authClass, databaseClass, errorClass, cacheClass } from '@bloomneo/appkit';

const auth     = authClass.get();
const database = await databaseClass.get();   // async
const error    = errorClass.get();
const cache    = cacheClass.get('users');     // optional namespace
```

## The one import rule (UIKit)

Always use the flat import from `@bloomneo/uikit`. Never deep-import unless optimising bundle size.

```ts
import { Button, DataTable, useTheme, toast } from '@bloomneo/uikit';
```

## Quick start (Bloom CLI)

```bash
npm install -g @bloomneo/bloom

bloom create my-app                  # web app
bloom create my-app userapp          # web app + full auth system
bloom create my-app desktop-basicapp # Electron desktop
bloom create my-app mobile-basicapp  # iOS + Android via Capacitor
```

## Feature-Based Component Architecture (FBCA)

Drop a folder inside `features/` — it's auto-discovered. No config, no registration.

```
src/api/features/invoice/
├── invoice.route.ts     # → /api/invoice (auto-mounted)
└── invoice.service.ts

src/web/features/invoice/pages/
├── index.tsx            # → /invoice
└── [id].tsx             # → /invoice/:id
```

## Auto-scaling (no code changes)

| Module   | Default     | Set env var                              | Upgrades to         |
|----------|-------------|------------------------------------------|---------------------|
| Cache    | in-memory   | `REDIS_URL`                              | Redis               |
| Queue    | in-memory   | `REDIS_URL`                              | Redis               |
| Events   | in-memory   | `REDIS_URL`                              | Redis pub/sub       |
| Storage  | local disk  | `AWS_S3_BUCKET` or `R2_BUCKET`           | S3 / Cloudflare R2  |
| Email    | console log | `SMTP_HOST` or `RESEND_API_KEY`          | SMTP / Resend       |

## Required env vars (minimum viable production)

```bash
BLOOM_AUTH_SECRET=<min 32 chars>           # JWT signing (AppKit auth)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...              # Prisma connection (AppKit database)
BLOOM_SECURITY_CSRF_SECRET=<min 32 chars> # CSRF protection (AppKit security)
```

## What NOT to do

- Never `new AuthClass()` — always `authClass.get()`
- Never `import { Button } from '@bloomneo/uikit/button'` as the primary import — use flat import
- Never read `process.env.X` directly in business code — use `configClass.get('section.key')`
- Never `throw new Error(...)` in Express routes — use `error.badRequest(...)`, `error.notFound(...)`, etc.
- Never chain `requireUserRoles()` after `requireApiToken()` — API tokens have no user roles

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/bloomneo
- npm org: https://www.npmjs.com/org/bloomneo
- Docs site: https://dev.bloomneo.com
- Philosophy: https://dev.bloomneo.com/philosophy/
